title: PreVillage: public-service knowledge before privilege
timing_notes: B01-B19 are mapped to recorded narration and Chirp2 word timing; B20 is editorial title-card tail after recorded VO.

typography.latin_display: Inter Black or equivalent local sans
typography.nepali: Noto Sans Devanagari / system Devanagari fallback
typography.caption: large, high contrast, no decorative letter spacing

global_motion.ambient: grain, subtle parallax on footage, slow push on photos and static cards
global_motion.intentional: hard cuts for evidence, mask reveal for Jiri, assembly animation for systems

rules.text_rule: On-screen text must add information beyond narration, except subtitles and title cards.
rules.asset_rule: Every ready asset in assets/manifest.json must be referenced by at least one beat.
validation_required: render start/mid/end still for every beat
verify every referenced asset is visible at least once
verify text is readable and not overlapping
verify animation evidence from start to mid frames

beats.name: Cold Open
beats.purpose: Establish lived public-service pain before any tech appears.
beats.narration_cue: Nepal has a problem.

layers.description: Recreated r/Nepal post card fills 80 percent of frame, slightly off-center over dark textured background.
layers.validation: reddit_card is visible, readable if textual, and supports the beat purpose.
layers.animation: slow 102 percent to 100 percent settle, expo out

layers.description: Large title text slams on top: Nepal has a problem.
layers.validation: hook_text is visible, readable if textual, and supports the beat purpose.
layers.animation: 2-frame anticipation then 10-frame slam, back easing

layers.description: Subtle red and blue circles from the card background, with 4 percent grain.
layers.validation: origin_texture is visible, readable if textual, and supports the beat purpose.

validation_criteria: At least three meaningful visual layers are present unless this is a deliberate title-card hold.
The hero element is identifiable within one second.
Referenced assets appear on screen and match the described purpose.
No text overlaps or falls outside the 1920x1080 frame.
Motion or editorial change is visible between start, middle, and end validation frames.

beats.name: Hidden Knowledge Has A Price
beats.purpose: Turn origin story into concrete numbers and route pain.
beats.narration_cue: Three weeks. Four offices. Four versions of the same form. Almost eight thousand rupees paid to middlemen.

layers.description: Four-office route graphic fills frame; office cards appear sequentially with route dots.
layers.validation: route_map is visible, readable if textual, and supports the beat purpose.
layers.animation: office cards reveal in 4 timed pops mapped to weeks/offices/forms/Rs 8k

layers.description: One paper copy moves between offices and receives red 'again' stamps at each redirect.
layers.validation: form_stamp is visible, readable if textual, and supports the beat purpose.
layers.animation: paper slides along route, stamp hit at each office

layers.description: 3 weeks, 4 offices, 4 forms, Rs 8k extra remain readable on left.
layers.validation: All four chips readable at 1080p.

layers.description: Tiny source note references CAMIS OCR plus IRD office pages.
layers.validation: source_note is visible, readable if textual, and supports the beat purpose.

validation_criteria: At least three meaningful visual layers are present unless this is a deliberate title-card hold.
The hero element is identifiable within one second.
Referenced assets appear on screen and match the described purpose.
No text overlaps or falls outside the 1920x1080 frame.
Motion or editorial change is visible between start, middle, and end validation frames.

beats.name: Not A One-Off
beats.purpose: Show middlemen/routing problem is broad, not one Reddit post.
beats.narration_cue: People are leveraging lack of proper pipeline and well documentation. I wanted to solve this.
overlay_text: People pay for information, not the service.

layers.description: Six news screenshots cycle as angled newspaper panels, each headline held 18-24 frames.
layers.validation: headline_montage is visible, readable if textual, and supports the beat purpose.
layers.animation: fast hard cuts, slight paper rotation, red underline sweeps across key middlemen words

layers.description: Jiri office laptop/forms footage interrupts headline montage twice.
layers.validation: office_form_cutaway is visible, readable if textual, and supports the beat purpose.
layers.animation: 2-frame flash cuts with handheld crop

layers.description: People pay for information, not the service.
layers.validation: cost_line is visible, readable if textual, and supports the beat purpose.
layers.text: People pay for information, not the service.
layers.position: bottom third on black solid strip

beats.name: E-Governance Gap
beats.purpose: Diagnose UX plus documented and undocumented knowledge.
beats.narration_cue: It's the gap every country transitioning into modern e-governance faces. Nepal has internet forms, but you still have to visit offices.

layers.description: Form/process reference video plays under warm translucent grade.
layers.validation: process_bg is visible, readable if textual, and supports the beat purpose.
layers.animation: slow 1.02x push, muted contrast

layers.description: Transparent overlay shows online form, print/check, office visit, redirect, repeat loop.
layers.validation: process_overlay is visible, readable if textual, and supports the beat purpose.
layers.animation: nodes assemble left to right; red loop draws last

layers.description: Documented knowledge and tacit knowledge labels stay visible at bottom.
layers.validation: knowledge_split is visible, readable if textual, and supports the beat purpose.

layers.description: Reference image informs line/card style; not necessarily visible in final frame.

beats.name: To Not Lay / To Lay
beats.purpose: Land the PreVillage/privi-lays concept: build on already learned UX.
beats.narration_cue: The internet of Nepal wasn't built for PDFs, HTML forms, and buttons. People already know how to speak, call, and use WhatsApp. I thought maybe we should start there.

layers.description: Left side shows portals, PDFs, forms, popups from government homepage montage.
layers.validation: bad_fit is visible, readable if textual, and supports the beat purpose.

layers.description: Right side shows WhatsApp and kiosk voice as familiar UX.
layers.validation: already_known is visible, readable if textual, and supports the beat purpose.

layers.description: Hen clip flashes for 'privi lays' wordplay, then exits quickly.
layers.validation: hen_flash is visible, readable if textual, and supports the beat purpose.
layers.animation: 0.5-2s flash depending edit rhythm

layers.description: TO NOT LAY vs TO LAY labels anchor comparison.
layers.validation: to_lay_labels is visible, readable if textual, and supports the beat purpose.
layers.text:
TO NOT LAY: PDFs / forms / buttons
TO LAY: voice / call / WhatsApp / kiosk

beats.name: Human Sentence Becomes Route
beats.purpose: Explain resolver job as turning natural language into route.
beats.narration_cue: A citizen does not begin with a portal URL. They ask naturally: Dharmadevi Nagarpalika, marriage registration, how do I do it? That is the job: turn a human sentence into a route.

layers.description: Tight phone recording shows marriage registration question in WhatsApp-like flow.

layers.description: WHERE, SERVICE, INTENT labels extract from sentence and connect to phone bubbles.
layers.validation: extraction is visible, readable if textual, and supports the beat purpose.

INTENT -> how do I do it?

beats.name: Ask First, Answer Second
beats.purpose: Show navigator behavior, not answering machine behavior.
beats.narration_cue: It can reason through messy ASR text, ask the next question, and compose from sources. The first job is not to answer, but to understand the case.

layers.description: Vague prompt becomes compact follow-up, then source-backed answer.
layers.validation: ask_first_ui is visible, readable if textual, and supports the beat purpose.

layers.description: Kiosk ASR shows messy speech becoming text.

beats.name: RAG Is Necessary But Not Enough
beats.purpose: Explain official source registry, crawlers, health checks, self-healing source maintenance.
beats.narration_cue: RAG alone is weak when truth is scattered across old websites, PDFs, office habits, and the memory of one contact officer. So I built a registry of 800+ government sources, crawlers, health checks, and a self-healing pipeline.

layers.description: Government sites flash rapidly to show source sprawl.
layers.validation: gov_sites is visible, readable if textual, and supports the beat purpose.

beats.name: Field Interviews Capture Practical Truth
beats.purpose: Show tacit office knowledge entering system through interviews.
beats.narration_cue: With this I also created a pipeline to capture tacit knowledge. Government officials can answer simple questions that never go documented.

beats.name: Review Turns Interview Into Source
beats.purpose: Prove human knowledge is reviewed and citeable.
beats.narration_cue: Those answers go through review, become practical sources, and can be cited later beside official government sources. Tacit knowledge is only unfair when it stays trapped in one office corridor.
overlay_text: interview -> review -> practical source -> cited answer

beats.name: Human Loop When Source Is Missing
beats.purpose: Show anti-hallucination and WhatsApp officer outreach loop.
beats.narration_cue: When PreVillage does not know, it should not hallucinate. It says what is missing, shows what sources it checked, and turns the citizen's question into an officer outreach message through the WhatsApp bridge. The answer is not invented. It is asked, reviewed, and folded back into the system for the next person.

layers.description: WhatsApp/outreach demo shows no authoritative source and drafted officer message.
layers.validation: no_source_flow is visible, readable if textual, and supports the beat purpose.

layers.description: Architecture diagram zooms into Human Loop and Practical Sources boxes.
layers.validation: human_loop_arch is visible, readable if textual, and supports the beat purpose.

layers.description: Admin review clip appears again as reply folding back into practical sources.
layers.validation: review_return is visible, readable if textual, and supports the beat purpose.

layers.description: no source? do not hallucinate; ask officer; review reply; fold back
layers.validation: no_hallucination_labels is visible, readable if textual, and supports the beat purpose.
layers.text:
no source?
do not hallucinate
ask officer
review reply
fold back

beats.name: Custom Voice Stack
beats.purpose: Show ASR/TTS grunt work and why voice matters.
beats.narration_cue: The internet UX of forms and buttons was not made with Nepali people in mind. Nepal is voice and language rich. So I trained our own TTS and ASR too.

beats.name: Voice Pipeline In Action
beats.purpose: Connect ASR, Gemma, RAG, and TTS as one pipeline.
beats.narration_cue: A citizen speaks. Our Nepali ASR transcribes. Gemma fixes the rough text and plans the intent. Retrieval finds official and practical sources. Our Nepali TTS speaks back.
overlay_text: speak -> ASR -> Gemma fix -> intent -> retrieve -> TTS

beats.name: Office Deployment
beats.purpose: Show office does not need L40 GPU; local device can run helpdesk onsite.
beats.narration_cue: On WhatsApp and kiosks in the office, help starts where people already ask for help. PreVillage is small enough to run on Raspberry Pi for compute and centralized enough to share and capture tacit knowledge.

beats.name: Definition And Close
beats.purpose: Ensure conclusion clearly defines PreVillage and lands the privilege line.
beats.narration_cue: Title-card close after recorded narration tail.

layers.description: Architecture diagram fades into simplified definition card.
layers.validation: architecture_simplify is visible, readable if textual, and supports the beat purpose.
layers.animation: slow pull-out then blur-free fade to title card

layers.description: Mountain or road footage provides quiet background texture behind final card.
layers.validation: mountain_close is visible, readable if textual, and supports the beat purpose.
layers.animation: slow Ken Burns, low contrast

layers.description: PreVillage definition appears in four short lines.
layers.validation: definition is visible, readable if textual, and supports the beat purpose.
layers.text:
PreVillage is a voice-first government-service navigator.
It asks first.
It checks official and practical sources.
It reaches officers when sources are missing.
It can run inside the office.

layers.description: PreVillage title and public-service knowledge before privilege close the video.
layers.validation: final_title is visible, readable if textual, and supports the beat purpose.
layers.text:
PreVillage
public-service knowledge before privilege

PreVillage exists because it is still a privilege
to navigate government services.
